relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: One final plague will break Pharaoh's refusal into expulsion.
The people are told to ask for silver and gold.
Favor rises among Egypt even while Pharaoh resists.
At midnight YHWH will move through Egypt.
The firstborn judgment touches throne, millstone, and beast.
A cry unlike any other will rise from Egypt.
Israel is held in distinction, down to human and beast.
Moses leaves Pharaoh in burning moral anger.
Pharaoh's refusal will not stop the signs from completing.
The wonders have been done, but Pharaoh still does not send.

contemplative_note: Exodus 11 is the terrible threshold: Pharaoh's firstborn violence returns as judgment, while favor among Egypt and distinction for Israel remain visible.
